title: exocarpos spathulatus
intro: Exocarpos spathulatus, also known as the drooping sheoak, is a small to medium-sized tree native to Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the family Santalaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Exocarpos spathulatus include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of the plant's leaves and bark can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before using it for medicinal purposes.
